elements.text: (iv) Consumer Credit Data; Limited Agency

elements.text: 1. With respect to any Closed Loan or Open Loan submitted to the System, the

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: which Consumer Credit Data is embedded (each a “Credit Report”) from a credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: repository or credit bureau (each a “Credit Reporting Agency”)

elements.text: 2. With respect to any Credit Report or other Consumer Credit Data retrieved as set
elements.label: text

elements.text: forth in this Section 2402.2(b)(iv), the Seller/Servicer hereby:

elements.text: i. Certifies that it has a permissible purpose under applicable State and federal fair

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Parties,
elements.label: text

elements.text: iii. Appoints the System as its limited agent for the sole purpose of having the System
elements.label: text

elements.text: effect such Credit Report and/or other Consumer Credit Data retrieval,
elements.label: text

elements.text: processing, assessment, storage and/or sharing with the Seller/Servicer and/or its
elements.label: text

elements.text: Related Third Parties, and
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: and its implementing regulations, and (b) any applicable State law and regulation

elements.text: (v) Excess capacity timesharing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac provides the System in part to assist the Seller/Servicer in assessing whether
elements.label: text

elements.text: an actual or potential Mortgage may be eligible for sale to Freddie Mac, which eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: is determined, among other things, by the Mortgage amount not exceeding the UPB
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount that Freddie Mac is permitted to purchase (any such Mortgage, an “Eligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage”).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The System has excess capacity beyond that required for identifying Eligible Mortgages,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the Seller/Servicer may, consistent with the Purpose and on a timesharing basis, use
elements.label: text

elements.text: the System to assess Mortgages other than Eligible Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi) Related Third Parties; Servicing transfers and sales; prospective purchasers
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Seller/Servicer may from time to time purchase or consider purchasing a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage from a Related Third Party that such Related Third Party processed through
elements.label: text

elements.text: the System.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer shall not rely on any Output provided to the Related Third Party
elements.label: text

elements.text: with respect to any such Mortgage but, rather, shall rely exclusively on any
elements.label: text

elements.text: representations and warranties made by the Related Third Party in connection with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller/Servicer’s actual or potential purchase of such Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. With respect to any Mortgage processed through the System for which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer seeks to or does effect a Concurrent Transfer of Servicing, Subsequent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transfer of Servicing or sale of such Mortgage, the Seller/Servicer may provide the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Output associated with such Mortgage to the Transferee Servicer or purchaser prior to
elements.label: text

elements.text: and after the Transfer of Servicing or sale; however, the Seller/Servicer must bind the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transferee Servicer or purchaser, as applicable, to non-disclosure obligations that are
elements.label: text

elements.text: substantially similar to those in the Guide to which the Seller/Servicer is bound with
elements.label: text

elements.text: respect to the Output.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Under no other circumstances shall the Seller/Servicer or any Related Third Party
elements.label: text

elements.text: provide any Output to any other third party except to a consumer to whom such
elements.label: text

elements.text: Output relates upon his or her request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Seller may share a Feedback Certificate with a third-party service provider in
elements.label: text

elements.text: connection with a securitization or with a prospective purchaser, provided the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following requirements are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: i. The Feedback Certificate relates to a Closed Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ii. The Feedback Certificate is shared solely for the purpose of the assessment of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage associated with the Feedback Certificate in order to determine
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility for a related pool/security or to make a purchase decision regarding the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: iii. The Feedback Certificate includes, or the Seller has provided to the third-party
elements.label: text

elements.text: service provider or prospective purchaser in writing concurrently with its sharing
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Feedback Certificate, the following notice:
elements.label: text

elements.text: This Feedback Certificate (i) is not a consumer report; (ii) does not constitute
elements.label: text

elements.text: an approval or denial by Loan Product Advisor or Freddie Mac of any
elements.label: text

elements.text: consumer loan application; (iii) may only be shared or distributed by the Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Product Advisor licensee, including the Seller/Servicer, as permitted in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Seller/Servicer Guide Sections 2401.1 and 2402.2(b)

elements.text: (iii) Loan Coverage Advisor; Source of Loan Coverage Advisor output
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. As more fully described in its Documentation, Loan Coverage Advisor calculates,
elements.label: text

elements.text: tracks and publishes the date on which the Seller/Servicer is relieved of certain
elements.label: text

elements.text: specified selling representations and warranties related to its underwriting of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower, Mortgaged Premises and any project in which the Mortgaged Premises is
elements.label: text

elements.text: located for Mortgages sold to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Loan Coverage Advisor Output is provided to the Seller/Servicer for informational
elements.label: text

elements.text: purposes to assist it in assessing its potential liabilities for representation and
elements.label: text

elements.text: warranty obligations to Freddie Mac, and is based on or derived directly from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: i. Loan-level delivery data provided by the original Seller/Servicer of each
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage in connection with Freddie Mac’s purchase of such Mortgage, and any
elements.label: text

elements.text: additional or corrective loan delivery data provided by the Seller/Servicer after
elements.label: text

elements.text: such Mortgage purchase, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ii. Investor accounting and reporting data provided by the Seller/Servicer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac as required by the Guide
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. All Loan Coverage Advisor Output is subject to final verification for accuracy by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac. This may result in a change in the date upon which certain of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer's representations and warranties expire under Section 1301.6.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. If the Seller/Servicer finds that any Loan Coverage Advisor Output is not consistent
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the Seller/Servicer’s loan data, including the loan-level delivery data, or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: investor accounting and reporting data for any Mortgage, the Seller/Servicer should
elements.label: text

elements.text: contact its Freddie Mac representative or Customer Service at 800-FREDDIE.

elements.text: (a) Scope and applicability

elements.text: to this System-Specific License, Section 2402.2 and the Master Systems License. Both

elements.text: Systems host certain remedy management information and quality control loan management

elements.text: data for Mortgages Freddie Mac owns or guarantees. As described in their respective
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documentation, Quality Control Advisor Plus reflects significant upgrades to features and
elements.label: text

elements.text: functionality sets within Quality Control Advisor, and both Systems shall be separately
elements.label: text

elements.text: accessible until Quality Control Advisor is retired at a future date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Immediately upon a User’s first access to either System on or after the Effective Date, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer shall be bound by this System-Specific License and the Master Systems
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: meaning set forth in the Glossary, the Master Systems License (Chapter 2401) or elsewhere
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) System access and use; loan classification
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in Section 2401.1(c), the Seller/Servicer shall use
elements.label: text

elements.text: the applicable System only in connection with Mortgages that Freddie Mac owns or
elements.label: text

elements.text: guarantees. Notwithstanding Section 2401.1(f) or any other applicable Purchase Document,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller/Servicer shall only access and use each System with Authentication Credentials
elements.label: text

elements.text: and other Confidential Means of Access (as defined in Section 1401.2) that are specifically
elements.label: text

elements.text: assigned or provisioned to an Authorized User by means of the System.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In connection with its quality control efforts, Freddie Mac may classify within each System
elements.label: text

elements.text: the loan and remedy management status of the Seller/Servicer Mortgages or otherwise assign
elements.label: text

elements.text: labels to such Mortgages. The Seller/Servicer understands and agrees that any such
elements.label: text

elements.text: classifications, labels or designations that Freddie Mac assigns to Mortgages tracked in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: System do not constitute a waiver by Freddie Mac of any rights and remedies available to it
elements.label: text

elements.text: under the Guide, any other Purchase Document or applicable law with respect to any such
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage, and Freddie Mac hereby expressly reserves all such rights and remedies.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 2403: Selling Mortgages through Loan
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Selling Advisor®
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2403.1: Selling Mortgages through Loan Selling
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Advisor®
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sale of Mortgages through Loan Selling Advisor®
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Post-Fund Data Correction tool
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Sale of Mortgages through Loan Selling Advisor®
elements.label: text

elements.text: This chapter sets forth requirements for the sale of Mortgages through Loan Selling
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advisor®.
elements.label: text

elements.text: By using Loan Selling Advisor, the Seller agrees to be bound by the Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: including, but not limited to, Chapter 1401, the applicable terms of this chapter, the terms of
elements.label: text

elements.text: any applicable Guide chapters, and any other relevant Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This Section 2403.1(a) is in addition to, and not in limitation of, Chapter 2401 and Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2402.2(e), which address the license terms applicable to the Sellers’ and Selling Agents’ use
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Loan Selling Advisor in connection with the sale of Mortgages to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Post-Fund Data Correction tool
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer must use the Post-Fund Data Correction tool, which is a “System” as defined
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 2401.1(b) to correct data on Seller/Servicer’s Mortgages previously entered into
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Selling Advisor, and must access such corrected data by means of the Post-Fund Data
elements.label: text

elements.text: Correction tool in accordance with Section 2401.1 and any directions provided by Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac, including, without limitation, any instructions set forth in the Documentation as defined
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 2401.1(b).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except to the extent consented to by Freddie Mac in writing, Seller/Servicer will not permit
elements.label: text

elements.text: any third parties to use the Post-Fund Data Correction tool, either directly or through
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2403.2: Mortgage Selling Agents and user roles
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Use of Selling Agents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ User roles
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Use of Selling Agents
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Definitions
elements.label: text

elements.text: Capitalized terms used herein, but not defined herein have the meanings set forth in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Glossary, Form 478, Secondary Market Advisor Selling Agent Agreement, Chapter 2401,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 2402 or elsewhere in the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Use of Selling Agent
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Secondary Market Advisor (SMA) and the Seller engaging the SMA must request
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s consent for the SMA to access and enter Loan Selling Advisor to perform
elements.label: text

elements.text: secondary market services for the Seller on the Seller’s behalf, as Selling Agent, for each
elements.label: text

elements.text: such Seller. The Seller and the SMA must comply with the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Every SMA must complete, execute and deliver to Freddie Mac a one-time Form 478. A
elements.label: text

elements.text: single Form 478 applies to all Sellers for which secondary market services are
elements.label: text

elements.text: performed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The SMA must enter, or have entered, into a services agreement with one or more
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller must designate duly authorized employees, as described in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2403.2(b), of the Selling Agent who have been assigned roles (“User Roles”) to
elements.label: text

elements.text: access, enter and use Loan Selling Advisor, on behalf of their employer, the Selling
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agent, to perform services for the Seller.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For cash or guarantor executions, the User Roles may be as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Closer/Shipper
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Exclusionary List
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Loan Manager
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Pipeline Manager; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Secondary Analyst
elements.label: text

elements.text: The specific roles for cash executions may be Cash SMO or Price Sheet Analyst. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: specific roles for guarantor executions may be Guarantor SMO, Security Wire
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assignor or Rate Sheet Analyst.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Immediately upon any of the SMA’s duly authorized employees’ first access to Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Selling Advisor, the Seller, by and through the SMA, as Selling Agent, will be bound by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the terms and conditions of Sections 2401.1 and 2402.2(e).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Selling Agent and Related Third Party
elements.label: text

elements.text: The SMA agrees that if Freddie Mac gives the SMA access to Loan Selling Advisor to
elements.label: text

elements.text: perform secondary market services for a Seller, as principal, the SMA must do so as such
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller’s duly authorized Selling Agent and a Related Third Party as defined in the Glossary.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Former Form 900SA exception
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any Seller that has: (i) an SMA that is performing secondary market services for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller in Loan Selling Advisor under a services agreement, (ii) completed, signed and
elements.label: text

elements.text: delivered to Freddie Mac either a form titled “Selling System Price Sheet Analyst User
elements.label: text

elements.text: ID Request Form” and/or Form 900SA and/or Form 900 that was delivered to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac on or before April 2, 2022, and (iii) authorized the SMA’s authorized employees to
elements.label: text

elements.text: perform secondary market services in Loan Selling Advisor (excluding the User Roles of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Setup Administrator or Setup Manager), is not immediately required to identify User
elements.label: text

elements.text: Roles as required in Section 2403.2(b) to Freddie Mac, unless and until it is necessary to
elements.label: text

elements.text: do so because the SMA and/or Seller need to: (A) add, modify or delete any SMA
elements.label: text

elements.text: authorized employees and/or (B) add or change secondary market services and “User
elements.label: text

elements.text: Roles.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Loan Selling Advisor access and security
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller and Selling Agent both acknowledge and agree that Loan Selling Advisor is
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s proprietary system and access to Loan Selling Advisor is provided by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac in its sole and absolute discretion.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller and Selling Agent also agree that each will keep the Seller-provided
elements.label: text

elements.text: information updated and current by making sure that the authorized users identified are
elements.label: text

elements.text: accurate and true. Changes to the authorized users identified must be made according to
elements.label: text

elements.text: this section and Sections 2401.1(f) and 2403.2(b).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller and Selling Agent represent and warrant to (and covenant with) Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: that only duly authorized employees of the SMA have been provided with their own
elements.label: text

elements.text: individual access to Loan Selling Advisor. Without limiting the application of any other
elements.label: text

elements.text: representation or warranty to (or covenant with) Freddie Mac under the Guide or any
elements.label: text

elements.text: other agreement, the Seller and Selling Agent further represent and warrant to (and
elements.label: text

elements.text: covenant with) Freddie Mac that the Seller and Selling Agent must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Safeguard User IDs and passwords to Loan Selling Advisor
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Adopt security measures to prevent sharing, loss, theft, or unauthorized disclosure or
elements.label: text

elements.text: use of User IDs and passwords to Loan Selling Advisor and information in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Selling Advisor
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Notify Freddie Mac as soon as practicable and, in any event, no later than within one
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business Day in the event:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Any authorized User’s (a) name change, (b) position/title change, (c) employment
elements.label: text

elements.text: termination, (d) authorization termination or (e) authorization expiration by
elements.label: text

elements.text: submitting changes per Section 2403.2(b) or by e-mailing Freddie Mac at
elements.label: text

elements.text: CSA_Operations@FreddieMac.com
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Of any loss, theft or unauthorized disclosure or use of any authorized User's User
elements.label: text

elements.text: ID and password to Loan Selling Advisor by e-mailing Freddie Mac at
elements.label: text

elements.text: CSA_Operations@FreddieMac.com; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Either the Seller or Selling Agent become aware of or has reason to believe that
elements.label: text

elements.text: an authorized user’s access to Loan Selling Advisor is no longer secure for any
elements.label: text

elements.text: reason by e-mailing Freddie Mac at CSA_Operations@FreddieMac.com
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi) Indemnification
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller hereby indemnifies and holds Freddie Mac and its respective directors,
elements.label: text

elements.text: officers, employees, agents, designees, successors and assigns harmless for any and all
elements.label: text

elements.text: liabilities, obligations, damages, penalties, actions, judgments, suits, disbursements,
elements.label: text

elements.text: losses, costs and expenses of any kind or nature. This includes reasonable legal fees and
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenses of counsel, court costs and costs of appeal which may be imposed on, incurred
elements.label: text

elements.text: by, or asserted against Freddie Mac as the result of any of Selling Agent’s errors,
elements.label: text

elements.text: omissions, negligence, breaches, gross negligence, willful misconduct or malicious
elements.label: text

elements.text: conduct when accessing, entering and/or using Loan Selling Advisor. This
elements.label: text

elements.text: indemnification applies without limiting other representations, warranties, or covenants
elements.label: text

elements.text: with Freddie Mac outlined in the Guide or any other agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vii) Form 478
elements.label: text

elements.text: Every SMA which is already in Loan Selling Advisor on behalf of a Seller must
elements.label: text

elements.text: complete, execute and deliver Form 478 to Freddie Mac. Failure to do so may result in
elements.label: text

elements.text: suspension of the SMA’s access to Loan Selling Advisor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) User roles
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) The Seller’s roles
elements.label: text

elements.text: In connection with use of Loan Selling Advisor®, the Seller must provide information to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac regarding authorized Loan Selling Advisor users and their designated user
elements.label: text

elements.text: roles. The Seller must submit its duly authorized employees, or, if using a Selling Agent,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller may designate the Selling Agent’s duly authorized employees as described in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 2403.2(a) by completing and submitting the Freddie Mac Access Manager
elements.label: text

elements.text: Administrator Registration form (i.e., FAM Form) or the Loan Advisor Registration form
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i.e., LAR Form).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon receipt of the FAM Form or LAR Form, Freddie Mac will provide the Seller with
elements.label: text

elements.text: user IDs and passwords for the designated individuals. (See Section 2403.4(b) with
elements.label: text

elements.text: respect to system security and the Seller’s responsibility.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any employee identified within the FAM Form or within the LAR Form as performing
elements.label: text

elements.text: the External Seller Setup Manager role must be designated by the Seller as an Authorized
elements.label: text

elements.text: Employee, authorized to provide Freddie Mac with instructions (or modify instructions)
elements.label: text

elements.text: to transfer funds or securities on the Seller’s behalf in connection with the sale of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages to Freddie Mac on the Seller’s:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 988SF, Certificate of Incumbency for a Bank, Savings Bank, Savings and Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Association, Credit Union, Corporation or Limited Liability Company
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 988SF-HFA, Certificate of Incumbency for Housing Finance Agency; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 989SF, Certificate of Incumbency for Limited Partnerships, as applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: (See Section 2201.1 for more information regarding Certificate of Incumbency
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements.) In addition, any Administrator designated as such on the FAM Form must
elements.label: text

elements.text: likewise be designated by the Seller in the applicable Certificate of Incumbency
elements.label: text

elements.text: identified above.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Document Custodian roles
elements.label: text

elements.text: In connection with use of Loan Selling Advisor, the Document Custodian must provide
elements.label: text

elements.text: information to Freddie Mac regarding Document Custodian’s authorized Loan Selling
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advisor users and their designated user roles by completing and submitting the FAM
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form or LAR Form.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Document Custodian must submit this information to Freddie Mac for each Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: with which the Document Custodian has established a custodian relationship, in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Section 2202.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon receipt of the completed FAM Form or LAR Form, Freddie Mac will assign user
elements.label: text

elements.text: roles in Loan Selling Advisor and issue user IDs and/or passwords to Authorized Users.
elements.label: text

elements.text: By executing the FAM or LAR Form, the Document Custodian represents and warrants
elements.label: text

elements.text: to, and covenants with, Freddie Mac that Freddie Mac may rely on the information
elements.label: text

elements.text: contained in the completed FAM Form and LAR Form. To add, remove or change a user
elements.label: text

elements.text: role, a Document Custodian must submit a FAM Form or LAR Form.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 2403.4(b) for requirements related to information security.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Warehouse Lender roles
elements.label: text

elements.text: In connection with use of Loan Selling Advisor, the Warehouse Lender must provide
elements.label: text

elements.text: information to Freddie Mac regarding the Warehouse Lender’s authorized Loan Selling
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advisor Administrators, other Authorized Employees and their designated user roles.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (See Section 6305.4(c)(ii) regarding the Warehouse Lender’s use of and access to Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Selling Advisor.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Warehouse Lender must submit this information to Freddie Mac by completing and
elements.label: text

elements.text: submitting Form 990SF, Agreement and Certificate of Incumbency: Warehouse Lender
elements.label: text

elements.text: and adding its Authorized Employees in Loan Selling Advisor. To add, remove or change
elements.label: text

elements.text: any user role, a Warehouse Lender must submit a FAM Form or LAR Form.
elements.label: text

elements.text: By executing Form 990SF, the Warehouse Lender represents and warrants to, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: covenants with, Freddie Mac that Freddie Mac may rely on the information on the Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: 990SF until the Warehouse Lender adds, modifies, or removes an Authorized Employee
elements.label: text

elements.text: by submitting a new Form 990SF. See Section 2403.4(b) for requirements related to
elements.label: text

elements.text: information security.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2403.3: Termination of eligibility (12/17/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Termination of eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Incorporation of Loan Selling Advisor® into the Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Termination of eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller acknowledges and agrees that at any time and in addition to any other remedies
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac is entitled to under the Purchase Documents, Freddie Mac may:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Revoke or suspend its consent for the Seller to use the provisions of this chapter or any
elements.label: text

elements.text: chapter providing terms for the sale of Mortgages to Freddie Mac using Loan Selling
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advisor®; and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Terminate the Seller’s eligibility to use any application or functionality within
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Selling Advisor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Incorporation of Loan Selling Advisor into the Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Selling Advisor is a Purchase Document. Any amendments, modifications, revisions,
elements.label: text

elements.text: changes, and/or updates made to Loan Selling Advisor by Freddie Mac will be automatically
elements.label: text

elements.text: incorporated into and made a part of the Purchase Documents whenever such amendments,
elements.label: text

elements.text: modifications, revisions, changes, and/or updates are published, disseminated or otherwise
elements.label: text

elements.text: made available to a Seller by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2403.4: Consent to conduct Electronic Transactions
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Consent to conduct electronic transactions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ System security
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Access to copies of transaction records
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Consent to conduct electronic transactions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller consents to conduct Electronic Transactions, as defined in Chapter 1401, with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac using Loan Selling Advisor® including, but not limited to, using Records,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Records and Electronic Signatures in accordance with and as defined in Chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1401 and agrees that the Seller will be bound by the terms and conditions of such Electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transactions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When using Loan Selling Advisor, the Seller will make on-screen selections by “clicking on”
elements.label: text

elements.text: certain designated items, icons or buttons to signify the Seller’s selection and/or agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: with various terms and conditions presented within Loan Selling Advisor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller’s Electronic Signature includes, but is not necessarily limited to, the process or
elements.label: text

elements.text: action of "clicking on" such designated items, icons or buttons.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) System security
elements.label: text

elements.text: Without limiting the provisions of Chapter 2401, Freddie Mac, in its sole discretion, will
elements.label: text

elements.text: provide Sellers, Document Custodians and Warehouse Lenders with, or require Sellers,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document Custodians and Warehouse Lenders to create, user IDs, passwords, personal
elements.label: text

elements.text: identification numbers and/or access codes, as applicable, to permit secure access to Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Selling Advisor®. Sellers, Document Custodians and Warehouse Lenders must adopt security
elements.label: text

elements.text: measures in accordance with Sections 1302.2, 1401.2(c), 1401.2(d), 2401.1 and
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2403.2(a)(vii), as applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a previously authorized user’s access must be terminated, the Seller, Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodian or Warehouse Lender must revoke the authorized user’s access as described in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 2403.2(b). Alternatively, if the Seller, Document Custodian or Warehouse Lender is
elements.label: text

elements.text: not enrolled in Freddie Mac Access Manager, they may request termination of access of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: previously authorized employee by notifying Freddie Mac at
elements.label: text

elements.text: CSA_Operations@FreddieMac.com.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will make commercially reasonable efforts to prevent the terminated employee
elements.label: text

elements.text: from accessing Loan Selling Advisor promptly after the initial request, but cannot guarantee
elements.label: text

elements.text: that it can prevent that employee from accessing Loan Selling Advisor before access has
elements.label: text

elements.text: been terminated.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Although Freddie Mac reserves the right to verify the information relating to any new
elements.label: text

elements.text: authorized user identified, neither the Seller, Document Custodian nor Warehouse Lender
elements.label: text

elements.text: may consider our failure to do so an act of negligence.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Access to copies of transaction records
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller acknowledges and agrees that its ongoing access to copies of Records and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Records related to any Electronic Transactions, as those terms are defined in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 1401, conducted by the Seller using Loan Selling Advisor® will be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Paper copies the Seller prints out using the Seller’s computer print capabilities and its
elements.label: text

elements.text: printer; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Electronic copies the Seller is able to download to its computer or copy to a disk
elements.label: text

elements.text: Accordingly, the Seller assumes responsibility for obtaining and retaining copies of any such
elements.label: text

elements.text: Records and Electronic Records for the Seller’s future reference. If the Seller is unable to
elements.label: text

elements.text: print, download or copy any of the Records or Electronic Records, the Seller may contact
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac for assistance in obtaining copies of the Electronic Records that Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: may have stored.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Single-Family Seller/Servicer Bulletin Chapter 2404
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 2404: System-Specific Licenses for
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Servicing Tools and Systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2404.1: System-Specific License for Specifications
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Scope and applicability
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Definitions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provision of the Specifications; development and use of the Interface
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Representations and warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Scope and applicability
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Specifications are licensed pursuant to this System-Specific License and the Master
elements.label: text

elements.text: Systems License.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Immediately upon a User’s first receipt of any Specifications on or after the Effective Date,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller/Servicer shall be bound by this System-Specific License and the Master Systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: License. This System-Specific License is incorporated into and made a part of the Master
elements.label: text

elements.text: Systems License by this reference. Any reference herein to the System-Specific License shall
elements.label: text

elements.text: be deemed to include this System-Specific License and the Master Systems License.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notwithstanding any applicable Purchase Document, the Servicer shall only access and use a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Tool with Authentication Credentials and other Confidential Means of Access that
elements.label: text

elements.text: are specifically assigned or provisioned to an Authorized User by means of a System.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Definitions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The definitions below apply for purposes of this System-Specific License. Capitalized terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: used but not defined in this System-Specific License have the meaning set forth in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Glossary, the Master Systems License (Chapter 2401) or elsewhere in the Guide:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Definitions related to the System-Specific License for specifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: C
elements.label: text

elements.text: Consumer Message A finding or message developed by the Seller/Servicer that is
elements.label: text

elements.text: readily understood by a consumer and is made available to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Single-Family Seller/Servicer Bulletin Chapter 2404
elements.label: text

elements.text: such consumer by the Seller/Servicer through the Lender
elements.label: text

elements.text: Platform
elements.label: text

elements.text: I
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interface One or more technical or user interfaces developed, owned
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or maintained by or on behalf of the Seller/Servicer that
elements.label: text

elements.text: enables Users to communicate with and use one or more
elements.label: text

elements.text: Systems through the transmission and receipt of data and other
elements.label: text

elements.text: information to and from, each such System by means of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lender Platform
elements.label: text

elements.text: L
elements.label: text

elements.text: L
elements.label: text

elements.text: L2C Specifications Any lender-to-consumer Specifications provided to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer that enable it to develop Consumer Messages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lender Platform A custom or other Servicing technology platform (including
elements.label: text

elements.text: any web-based application, software or system, as well as each
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interface and all releases, upgrades and other modifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: thereto) developed, owned and/or maintained by or on behalf
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2405.1: Expense reimbursement process (12/17/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Process for requesting expense reimbursement and for receiving reimbursement of expenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Use of PAID (Payments Automated Intelligent and Dynamic) by Related Third Parties and
elements.label: text

elements.text: process for Related Third Parties to receive reimbursement of expenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer bound by use of PAID
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Process for requesting expense reimbursement and for receiving reimbursement of
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must submit a request for reimbursement via PAID (Payments Automated
elements.label: text

elements.text: Intelligent and Dynamic) (available at https://sf.freddiemac.com/working-with￾us/servicing/overview) in accordance with the requirements of this chapter:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any Servicer submitting an expense reimbursement request for the first time must obtain
elements.label: text

elements.text: access to PAID through Access Manager
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicers may access the PAID Online Help Section for additional information and
elements.label: text

elements.text: assistance with PAID
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be reimbursed for expenses, Servicers must authorize Freddie Mac to make such
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments and receive debits via Automated Clearing House (ACH) credit entries into their
elements.label: text

elements.text: commercial checking accounts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must complete and execute Form 1132, in order to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Authorize receipt of ACH credit entries; and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Update previously submitted ACH credit account instructions
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 1132 must be submitted to Freddie Mac as an Electronic Record (as defined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1401.1(b)), using a Portable Document Format (PDF) (or other Electronic Record format
elements.label: text

elements.text: commonly used in the mortgage industry), that has been completed and contains the copy or
elements.label: text

elements.text: representation of the pen and ink signature of the Authorized Employee (such copy or
elements.label: text

elements.text: representation of the Authorized Employee’s signature shall be such Authorized Employee’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: adopted Electronic Signature as defined in Section 1401.1(b).) Servicers may submit the
elements.label: text

elements.text: completed form by either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Email: Attach to an e-mail and deliver to Freddie Mac at
elements.label: text

elements.text: CashCollections@FreddieMac.com; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Upload: Submit through the Freddie Mac eBill system
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may submit ACH credit-related questions or concerns to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Expense@FreddieMac.com.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The employee authorized to execute Form 1132 on the Servicer’s behalf must be designated
elements.label: text

elements.text: as an “Authorized Employee” on the Servicer’s applicable certificate of incumbency form:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 988SF; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 989SF
elements.label: text

elements.text: Before a Servicer may receive expense reimbursements and incentive payments via ACH
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit entries, the Servicer must have completed the certificate of incumbency (“COI”)
elements.label: text

elements.text: process requirements in Section 2201.1, and Freddie Mac must have accepted the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: COI and all other required forms and documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Use of PAID by Related Third Parties and process for Related Third Parties to receive
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimbursement of expenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Access to PAID for Related Third Parties
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may grant PAID access to one or more of its vendors which provide
elements.label: text

elements.text: services in connection with Mortgages serviced for Freddie Mac. Each vendor is a
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Related Third Party” within the meaning of the Guide. Related Third Parties are allowed
elements.label: text

elements.text: access to PAID for the purposes of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Submitting requests for pre-approval (RPAs) via the RPA functionality in PAID
elements.label: text

elements.text: pursuant to Section 9701.1(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Submitting expenses for Servicer reimbursement based on the expense and income
elements.label: text

elements.text: codes, subject to any associated expense limits, listed in Exhibits 57, 57A, 57B and
elements.label: text

elements.text: 74, Expense and Income Codes for Expense Reimbursement Requests
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Freddie Mac, at any time and in its sole discretion, may terminate a Related Third
elements.label: text

elements.text: Party’s access to PAID and/or direct the Servicer to terminate a Related Third Party’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: access.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Servicer requirements for Related Third Party use of PAID
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must comply with the following requirements related to use of PAID by a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Related Third Party:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must ensure that Related Third Parties sign and comply with their
elements.label: text

elements.text: separate, independent contracts under Sections 2401.1(e)(iii). The Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintain auditable records of such contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must monitor its Related Third Party to ensure compliance with all
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable requirements of the Guide and any other Purchase Documents and
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable law and have written policies and procedures in place for doing so
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must have written policies and procedures in place that document its
elements.label: text

elements.text: criteria for selecting Related Third Party and how the Servicer will monitor the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Related Third Party’s use of PAID
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must periodically review and analyze the types and dollar amounts of
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenses requested by Related Third Parties to ensure compliance with all applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For property preservation services, if Freddie Mac requires that competitive bids be
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtained from multiple vendors, the Servicer is responsible for obtaining the multiple
elements.label: text

elements.text: bids and keeping records of the bids.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Vendor COI process
elements.label: text

elements.text: Before a vendor may receive expense reimbursements and incentive payments, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: vendor must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Complete the COI process requirements in Section 2201.1; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure Freddie Mac must have accepted the vendor’s COI and all other required
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Reimbursement and incentive payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be reimbursed for expenses and paid incentives (see Section 9204.3(a) regarding
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer compensation for alternatives to foreclosure), Related Third Parties must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Authorize Freddie Mac to make such payments and receive debits via ACH credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: entries into their commercial checking accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Agree to authorize Freddie Mac to initiate ACH drafts from accounts designated on
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 98 for overpayments of expenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: The employee authorized to execute Form 98 on the vendor’s behalf must be designated
elements.label: text

elements.text: as an “Authorized Employee” on the vendor’s Form 99V.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Authorization process for ACH transactions
elements.label: text

elements.text: To authorize receipt of ACH credit entries or to make changes to ACH credit account
elements.label: text

elements.text: instructions previously provided, vendors must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Complete and execute Form 98; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Submit the form to Freddie Mac as an Electronic Record (as defined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: To submit your authorization documents for review, please adhere to the following steps:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Complete Form 98 as a PDF (or other Electronic Record format commonly used in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the mortgage industry) that contains the copy or representation of the pen and ink
elements.label: text

elements.text: signature of the Authorized Employee (such copy or representation of the Authorized
elements.label: text

elements.text: Employee’s signature shall be such Authorized Employee’s adopted Electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: Signature as defined in Section 1401.1(b))
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Attach the completed form to an e-mail and deliver to Freddie Mac at the following e￾mail address: Counterparty_Authorization@FreddieMac.com
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Servicer bound by use of PAID
elements.label: text

elements.text: By virtue of Servicer’s use of PAID, the Servicer agrees and is deemed to be bound by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: provisions of: (i) Chapter 1401; (ii) Section 2401.1, (iii) this Chapter 2405; (iv) Chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2405.2: Electronic Transactions and access (12/17/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Consent to conduct Electronic Transactions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Access to copies of Electronic Transaction Records
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ User access requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following terms used in this Chapter are defined in Chapter 1401:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: E
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Record A “Record” created, generated, sent, communicated, received,
elements.label: text

elements.text: or stored by “Electronic” means, as defined in the “UETA”
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or “E-SIGN.” The term also includes a paper document
elements.label: text

elements.text: converted into an Electronic Record.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Signature An “Electronic” sound, symbol or process attached to, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: logically associated with, a contract or other “Record” and
elements.label: text

elements.text: executed or adopted by a person with the intent to sign the
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Record,” as defined in the “UETA” and/or “E-SIGN”
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Transaction An action or set of actions occurring between two or more
elements.label: text

elements.text: persons relating to the conduct of business, commercial, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: governmental affairs, using “Electronic” means, as defined in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the “UETA” and/or “E-SIGN”
elements.label: text

elements.text: R
elements.label: text

elements.text: Record Information that is inscribed on a tangible medium or that is
elements.label: text

elements.text: stored in an electronic or other medium and is retrievable in
elements.label: text

elements.text: perceivable form as defined in the “UETA” and/or “E-SIGN.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Record may be a paper or an “Electronic” document.
elements.label: text

elements.text: S
elements.label: text

elements.text: System All computers, servers, fax machines, other Electronic devices,
elements.label: text

elements.text: hardware, websites, internet, private networks, telephone lines
elements.label: text

elements.text: or wireless communications, together with software
elements.label: text

elements.text: applications, security measures, proprietary coding, interfaces
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or connectivity used to create, present, sign, transfer,
elements.label: text

elements.text: transmit, send, submit, deliver, receive, retrieve maintain
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or store Records, Electronic Records or Electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: Signatures in order to engage in and/or conduct Electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transactions
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Consent to conduct Electronic Transactions
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer consents to conduct Electronic Transactions with Freddie Mac using PAID
elements.label: text

elements.text: including, but not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Records
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Electronic Records; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Electronic Signatures
elements.label: text

elements.text: All such transactions shall be conducted in accordance with Chapter 1401 and the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: agrees to be legally bound by the terms and conditions of such Electronic Transactions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When using PAID, the Servicer will make on-screen selections by “clicking on” certain
elements.label: text

elements.text: designated terms, items, icons or buttons to signify Servicer’s agreement with various terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: and conditions presented within PAID. Servicer’s Electronic Signature, includes, but is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: necessarily limited to, the process or action of “clicking on” such designated items, icons or
elements.label: text

elements.text: buttons.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Access to copies of Electronic Transaction Records
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer acknowledges and agrees that its ongoing access to copies of Records and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Records related to any Electronic Transactions conducted by Servicer using PAID
elements.label: text

elements.text: will be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Paper copies Servicer prints out using its Systems, print capabilities and its printer; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Electronic copies Servicer is able to download to its computer or copy to a disk or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic device
elements.label: text

elements.text: Accordingly, the Servicer assumes responsibility for obtaining and retaining copies of any
elements.label: text

elements.text: such Records and Electronic Records for Servicer’s future reference. If the Servicer is unable
elements.label: text

elements.text: to print, download or copy any of the Records or Electronic Records, the Servicer may
elements.label: text

elements.text: contact Customer Service at 800-FREDDIE and select option four for Servicing for
elements.label: text

elements.text: assistance in obtaining copies of the Electronic Records.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) User access requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer acknowledges and agrees that Freddie Mac has provided Servicer with written or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic, as defined in Chapter 1401, information that describes the minimum hardware,
elements.label: text

elements.text: software, browser and other internet connectivity requirements necessary to conduct
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Transactions, as defined in Chapter 1401, with Freddie Mac using PAID.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: In managing access to BPODirect Servicers must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure that only active employees of the Servicer or its Related Third Parties with
elements.label: text

elements.text: a need to use BPODirect are provided access, which shall be facilitated and
elements.label: text

elements.text: managed through Access Manager pursuant to Section 2401.1(f)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Monitor any Related Third Party it engages to perform loss mitigation activities
elements.label: text

elements.text: including the use of BPODirect in connection with the Servicing of Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages, and ensure compliance with all applicable requirements of the Guide
elements.label: text

elements.text: and any other Purchase Documents and applicable law, and have written policies
elements.label: text

elements.text: and procedures in place for doing so
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Obtaining a property value via BPODirect
elements.label: text

elements.text: When Freddie Mac requires that a property value be obtained through BPODirect,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must obtain the property value via Freddie Mac Gateway®.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer represents and warrants that all information it provides for the purpose
elements.label: text

elements.text: of obtaining the property value, including the address of the Mortgaged Premises, is
elements.label: text

elements.text: true, complete and accurate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a Servicer accesses BPODirect, the website may display an automated value if
elements.label: text

elements.text: one is available:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. If an automated value is not available in BPODirect or through another permitted
elements.label: text

elements.text: source (e.g., Automated Valuation Model (AVM) report), or the MI requires a
elements.label: text

elements.text: BPO/property valuation, the Servicer must submit a new property valuation
elements.label: text

elements.text: request to Freddie Mac (i.e., order a new BPO from BPODirect), if necessary.
elements.label: text

elements.text: There is no cost associated with automated values obtained through BPODirect,
elements.label: text

elements.text: however there is a cost associated with ordering a valuation (BPO or appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: reports). See Section 9202.4 for costs associated with ordering valuations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. If Freddie Mac requires a BPO for the situation the Servicer is evaluating, then,
elements.label: text

elements.text: unless otherwise noted below, the Servicer must take the additional step of
elements.label: text

elements.text: ordering a new BPO from BPODirect. Once the BPO valuation is complete, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: BPO value will be available to the Servicer in BPODirect.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notwithstanding the requirements above, if the Servicer has previously obtained a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac-compliant property valuation (i.e., Freddie Mac-provided BPO, Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac-provided appraisal or an appraisal obtained in compliance with Topic 5600), the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must use the Freddie Mac-compliant property valuation provided it meets
elements.label: text

elements.text: the property valuation requirements for the specific relief or workout option. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer may not obtain a new property valuation in this circumstance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: If the Mortgage is covered by mortgage insurance, the Servicer must ensure
elements.label: text

elements.text: that the property value it obtains is based on a property valuation type that is
elements.label: text

elements.text: consistent with the MI’s requirements.
elements.label: text
